2606.07451.title: TEVI: Text-Conditioned Editing of Visual Representations via Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Vision-Language Alignment
2606.07451.paper_summary: Vision-language models such as CLIP are highly useful for diverse tasks due to their shared image-text embedding space. Despite this, the image and text embeddings are often poorly aligned, affecting downstream performance. Recent work has shown that this can be attributed to an information imbalance: images contain more information than their captions describe. In this work, we propose TEVI, a framework that uses captions as a signal for what to retain from image embeddings. Specifically, we use sparse autoencoders to disentangle image embeddings and train a masking module to selectively reconstruct the embedding based on a given caption. In a controlled setup with synthetic captions, we show that TEVI is effective at preserving caption-described attributes while discarding others. By applying TEVI to CLIP models trained on natural images, we further achieve improved retrieval performance across coarse-grained short-caption (MS COCO, Flickr) and fine-grained long-caption (IIW, DOCCI) benchmarks, with stronger gains on richer captions, and improved robustness on the RoCOCO benchmark.
2606.07451.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型如 CLIP 由於其共享的圖像-文本嵌入空間，對於多樣化的任務非常有用。<lb/>儘管如此，圖像和文本的嵌入經常對齊不佳，影響下游性能。<lb/>最近的研究顯示，這可以歸因於信息不平衡：圖像包含的信息超過其標題所描述的內容。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了 TEVI，一個使用標題作為從圖像嵌入中保留內容信號的框架。<lb/>具體而言，我們使用稀疏自編碼器來解開圖像嵌入，並訓練一個遮罩模塊，以根據給定的標題選擇性地重建嵌入。<lb/>在一個使用合成標題的受控設置中，我們顯示 TEVI 在保留標題描述的屬性方面是有效的，同時丟棄其他屬性。<lb/>通過將 TEVI 應用於在自然圖像上訓練的 CLIP 模型，我們進一步在粗粒度短標題（MS COCO、Flickr）和細粒度長標題（IIW、DOCCI）基準上實現了改進的檢索性能，對於更豐富的標題獲得了更強的增益，並在 RoCOCO 基準上提高了穩健性。
2606.07451.author: Sweta Mahajan et.al.
2606.07451.authors: Sweta Mahajan, Sukrut Rao, Jiahao Xie, Alexander Koller, Bernt Schiele
